top_signals.title: Bank of England launches policy statement and draft rules on regulating systemic stablecoins
top_signals.summary: The Bank of England has today published its policy statement and draft Code of Practice (rules) for systemic stablecoin issuers.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Making Miliband chancellor would be a ‘mistake’, Trump officials warn
top_signals.summary: Senior officials in president Trump’s administration have privately warned that appointing Ed Miliband as chancellor would be a “mistake”, as speculation over who will take over from Rachel Reeves grows. US officials told their counterparts in Britain, as well as senior Labour figures, that they were concerned about Miliband’s opposition to new oil and gas [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Labour defends Burnham’s ‘very powerful’ No 10 North plans
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham’s proposed ‘No 10 North’ would lead to a “very powerful reorganisation of government”, deputy Labour leader Lucy Powell has said. Her words come as ministers push ahead with one of the biggest overhauls of English local government in decades before Burnham is expected to enter Downing Street later this month, with Labour arguing [...]

top_signals.title: Envoy hails growing Japan-Philippines economic and security ties
top_signals.summary: Bilateral ties are now at "the highest point ever," following Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s state visit to Japan in late May, the Philippines' ambassador said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: National Payments Vision
top_signals.summary: The government has published the National Payments Vision, setting out its ambitions for the UK’s payments sector to deliver world-leading payments and support the growth mission.

top_signals.title: Google loses final appeal to overturn €4.1 billion EU fine
top_signals.summary: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has dismissed Google's final appeal against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) antitrust fine over the company's use of Android to promote its Chrome browser and search service. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
top_signals.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.

alerts.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
alerts.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.
